# 🎯 Task 01.7: Persian Morphological Analysis & Stemming

## 📅 Sprint Info
- **Duration:** 5 days
- **Milestone:** M0 - Foundation
- **Priority:** P0 (Critical Path for Persian search quality)
- **Depends On:** Task 01.3 (Script-Specific Processing) ✅
- **Blocks:** Task 02.1 (BM25), Task 05.3 (Subword Embeddings)
- **Assignee:** TBD

## 🎬 What You'll Build
Implement a Persian-specific morphological analysis pipeline: stemmer, lemmatizer, prefix/suffix stripping, verb conjugation normalization, and compound word handling. This is a critical differentiator from generic Unicode normalization — Persian has rich morphology (agglutinative prefixes/suffixes, verbal inflection, ezafe constructions, derivational morphology) that generic processing cannot handle, directly impacting recall and ranking quality.

## 🧠 Why This Matters
Without morphological analysis, the query "کتاب‌های جدید" (new books) will not match documents containing "کتاب" (book), "کتابی" (a book), or "کتاب‌ها" (books). This significantly hurts recall. Persian morphology is complex:
- **Plural forms:** -ها، -ان (کتاب → کتاب‌ها، معلم → معلمان)
- **Verb conjugation:** رفتن → می‌روم، رفتم، می‌رفت، خواهم رفت
- **Ezafe construction:** کتابِ فارسی (book of/in Persian)
- **Clitics:** ـم، ـت، ـش (possessive suffixes)
- **Agglutinative prefixes:** می‌، نمی‌، بی‌، ناـ
- **Derivational suffixes:** ـگر، ـکار، ـمند، ـی

## 📋 Daily Breakdown

### Day 1: Corpus Analysis & Rule Design
- [ ] Analyze top-10K Persian query terms from existing corpus
- [ ] Document all common suffix/prefix patterns (≥50 patterns)
- [ ] Map verb stem forms (present, past) for top-2000 Persian verbs
- [ ] Collect noun plural patterns and irregular forms
- [ ] Review Hazm, Parsivar, and Stanza Persian models for reference
- [ ] Choose implementation strategy: rule-based + dictionary hybrid
- [ ] Create test dataset of 500 word-form → lemma pairs

### Day 2: Stemmer Implementation
- [ ] Implement suffix-stripping stemmer (Lovins/Porter style, Persian-adapted)
- [ ] Add plural normalization (-ها, -ان, -گان, -ات)
- [ ] Handle enclitic stripping (ـم, ـت, ـش, ـمان, ـتان, ـشان)
- [ ] Add ezafe removal (ـِ connector)
- [ ] Implement prefix stripping (می‌, نمی‌, بی‌, ناـ, هم‌)
- [ ] Handle ZWNJ-attached morphemes (requires Task 01.3 output)
- [ ] Unit tests: 200+ word pairs
- [ ] Benchmark: <0.5ms per token

### Day 3: Lemmatizer & Verb Normalization
- [ ] Build present-to-past stem dictionary (top-5000 verbs)
- [ ] Implement verb conjugation stripping (personal endings)
- [ ] Normalize causative and passive forms
- [ ] Handle compound verbs (رفتن → برو/رفت; کردن compounds)
- [ ] Integrate dictionary-based lemmatization (Wiktionary + DadeganPersian)
- [ ] Fallback: stemmer output when lemma not found
- [ ] Unit tests: 200+ verb conjugation cases

### Day 4: Integration with Query and Index Pipeline
- [ ] Create Python module `PersianMorphology` with `stem()` and `lemmatize()`
- [ ] Build C++ wrapper via pybind11 (see Task 01.6 pattern)
- [ ] Apply at **index time**: store both original token and stem/lemma
- [ ] Apply at **query time**: expand query with morphological variants
- [ ] Add Redis cache for stemmed/lemmatized forms
- [ ] Integration test: end-to-end query → morphological expansion → BM25 retrieval
- [ ] Performance: <1ms per query for 5-token average Persian query

### Day 5: Evaluation & Quality Validation
- [ ] Build evaluation harness: stemming precision/recall on test set
- [ ] Validate on 150+ Persian query pairs (same-meaning different-form)
- [ ] Measure recall improvement vs no-morphology baseline (target: +15%)
- [ ] Check for over-stemming errors (false merge rate <5%)
- [ ] Test with Persian names, brands, technical terms (should NOT be stemmed)
- [ ] Add named-entity detection to bypass morphological analysis
- [ ] Document edge cases and known failures
- [ ] Final benchmark: <1ms per token at P95

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Stemmer accuracy ≥90% on test dataset (500 word-form → stem pairs)
- [ ] Lemmatizer accuracy ≥85% for top-5000 word forms
- [ ] Verb conjugation normalization ≥88% accuracy
- [ ] Recall improvement: ≥15% on morphological query pairs vs baseline
- [ ] Over-stemming rate: <5% (words incorrectly merged)
- [ ] Named entities and proper nouns NOT stemmed (≥95% pass rate)
- [ ] Latency: <1ms per token (P95), <0.5ms median
- [ ] Both index-time and query-time application implemented
- [ ] C++ integration via pybind11 working

## 🧪 Testing Checklist
- [ ] 500 noun form → lemma pairs (various plurals, clitics)
- [ ] 200 verb conjugation → infinitive pairs
- [ ] 100 prefix-stripped forms
- [ ] 50 compound word cases
- [ ] 50 proper noun / brand name tests (must NOT be altered)
- [ ] 150 query-level recall tests (query variants → same results)
- [ ] Precision test: no query over-expansion
- [ ] Performance benchmark at P50/P95/P99

## 📊 Required Evidence
- Stemming/lemmatization accuracy table on 500-item test set.
- Recall improvement on 150 morphological query pairs vs no-morphology BM25 baseline.
- Over-stemming error report: list of falsely merged word pairs.
- Named entity bypass test: show that "تهران", "گوگل", "علی" are not modified.
- P95 latency measurement for both index-time and query-time processing.

## 🎉 Celebration Criteria (Definition of Done)
✅ **Demo Ready:** Show query "کتاب‌های جدید" returning results containing "کتاب", "کتابی", "کتابهای"
✅ **Metric Met:** ≥15% recall improvement, <5% over-stemming rate
✅ **Integration:** C++ query pipeline calls morphological analysis transparently
✅ **Performance:** <1ms per token P95

**🎊 Celebration Moment:** Persian morphological analysis bridges the vocabulary gap — same meaning, different form, same search result!

## 📦 Deliverables
- `src/python/text_processing/persian_morphology.py` (600 lines)
- `src/python/text_processing/verb_stems.py` (verb dictionary, 400 lines)
- `src/python/text_processing/suffix_rules.py` (rule tables, 300 lines)
- `include/text_processing/PersianMorphology.h` (C++ header via pybind11, 80 lines)
- `src/text_processing/PersianMorphology.cpp` (C++ wrapper, 150 lines)
- `data/persian_lemma_dictionary.json` (lemma dictionary, ~50K entries)
- `tests/test_persian_morphology.py` (500+ test cases)
- `docs/persian-morphology-guide.md`

## 🔗 Key Libraries
- **Hazm** (Python): Persian NLP toolkit, use as reference/baseline
- **Parsivar** (Python): Persian text normalizer and stemmer
- **Stanza** (Stanford NLP): Has Persian model for POS/lemma
- **DadeganPersian**: Open Persian morphological dictionary

## ⚠️ Persian-Specific Edge Cases
| Pattern | Example | Handling |
|---------|---------|---------|
| ZWNJ plural | کتاب‌ها | Strip ‌ها after ZWNJ normalization |
| Space plural | کتاب ها | Handled by tokenizer + stemmer |
| Possessive clitic | کتابش | Strip ـش (his/her book) |
| Ezafe | کتابِ فارسی | Remove ِ ezafe marker |
| Verb copula | رفتم/رفتی/رفت | All → رفت (past stem) |
| Causative | نشاندن/نشاندم | Normalize to نشاند |
| Compound verb | درس خواندن | Handle as unit, don't split |
| Brand name | اسنپ | Do not stem |
| Persian number | سه‌تایی | Detect and preserve |
